notes: Create, list, edit, delete, and clone agents via ship agents subcommands. Implemented in cli-framework with full CRUD operations, tested, and documented.

Agent definitions in JSONC (and TOML legacy) with identity, skills refs, MCP servers, plugins, permissions, rules, and provider_settings. Full JSONC parser in agent_parser.rs with validation and schema support.

AgentProfile TOML/JSONC compiled into provider-native subagent files for Claude, Gemini, Cursor, and Codex. Tested in compile/agents.rs with 6+ test cases covering all providers. OpenCode support also exists.
Agents can target specific providers (via profile.providers list) or all providers when the list is empty. Implemented in profile_targets_provider() with explicit tests for both cases.
Per-agent permission overrides with preset reference, tools_allow, tools_deny, and tools_ask lists. Enforced at compile time via permissions.rs with violation detection and actionable error messages.
Per-agent model override compiled to each provider's model field. Implemented in compile/{claude,gemini,cursor,codex}.rs with provider-specific field handling.

Per-provider settings passthrough (claude, codex, gemini, cursor, opencode) with Ship-managed key exclusions (permissions, hooks, model, env). Schema validation enforces exclusions. Tested in realistic_tests.rs.

Inline rules appended to agent's YAML frontmatter in provider-native agent files. Compiled into Claude/Gemini/Cursor agent bodies. Rules are part of ProfileRules struct with inline field populated in all 15 active agents.
Plugin installation intent with project or user scope, emitted as manifest for CLI execution. Implemented in PluginRefs and PluginsManifest types. Compiler emits plugins_manifest in CompileOutput.

JSON Schema at schemas/agent.schema.json with 200+ lines covering all agent properties and upstream provider schema refs for editor autocompletion. Schema version 1 supports forward-compatible migrations.

Hidden ship agents log command appends timestamped entries to .ship/agent.log. Implemented in apps/ship-studio-cli/src/agent.rs with RFC3339 timestamps. Used by skills to log progress during sessions.

Spec exists (agent-hooks/spec.md) defining per-agent hooks that merge with project-level hooks. Schema describes the desired structure. However, AgentProfile struct in agent_profile.rs does NOT yet have a hooks field. The feature is designed but not implemented in the compiler pipeline.

Session events table and capture infrastructure exists (db/session_events.rs). Basic tool call counting works (tool_call_count in workspace_session). The spec (agent-analytics/spec.md) defines granular session_event table, timeline queries, and agent stats. However, get_session_timeline and get_agent_stats MCP tools are NOT implemented in shipd. Token counting is partial.

Push notifications work on Claude (Notification hook + MCP ship/event notification) and Codex (patched). Gemini has Notification trigger mapped in compile/gemini.rs but untested with actual providers. Cursor Notification trigger explicitly unmapped (returns empty array). OpenCode not implemented. Need testing/hardening for Gemini and Cursor.
Ship's own agent runtime not dependent on third-party providers. Long-term roadmap. No code exists yet. Would require significant architecture work to replace provider delegation.

Spec exists (agent-quality-audit/spec.md) with detailed audit framework and decision matrix for 22 agents. Audit not yet executed. Decision point decisions outlined: keep 14 agents, cut 6-8, merge 2-3 across clusters (React, Review, Rust, Lane stubs). Legacy .toml files still present alongside .jsonc.
